perturbation.original_text: Any required notice shall be effective two business days after it is sent by a recognized international courier service to the address of the other Party stated in this Agreement, or such other address as shall be notified to the other Party in writing, and any receipt issued by the courier service shall be conclusive evidence of the fact and date of sending of any such notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Any required notice shall be effective five business days after it is sent by a recognized international courier service to the address of the other Party stated in this Agreement, or such other address as shall be notified to the other Party in writing, and any receipt issued by the courier service shall not be conclusive evidence of the fact and date of sending of any such notice.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the notice period from two to five business days and removing receipt issued by the courier service shall be conclusive, creates contradiction with instances that might rely on the original notice period. This is a contradiction if a section of the text requires action based within a short window, which would make it difficult to properly act on it or have any way to make sure it was properly delivered. In this case, it would be Section 1 with the timeframe required to accept a proposed offer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the Offering Party is required to offer the Receiving Party a Development Opportunity pursuant to this Section 1, the Offering Party must deliver written notice to the Receiving Party, together with reasonable due diligence information in the Offering Party's possession to enable the Receiving Party to evaluate the Development Opportunity (collectively, the "Offer Notice"). The Receiving Party will have ten (10) business days after receiving the Offer Notice to notify the Offering Party whether the Receiving Party exercises its right of first offer for that Development Opportunity.
perturbation.justification: The change increases the notice period from two to five business days and removes the conclusive evidence provision for courier receipts. This directly contradicts Section 1, which allows only ten business days for the Receiving Party to respond to a Development Opportunity Offer Notice. The increased notice period reduces the time available for due diligence and response, and the lack of conclusive proof of delivery introduces uncertainty and potential delays, making it difficult to comply with the ten-day deadline. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The arbitrator(s) shall have the power to grant any remedy or relief that they deem just and equitable, including injunctive relief, whether interim and/or final, and any provisional measures ordered by the arbitrator(s) may be specifically enforced by any court of competent jurisdiction. Each Party hereto retains the right to seek interim measures from a judicial or other governmental authority, and any such request shall not be deemed incompatible with the agreement to arbitrate or a waiver of the right to arbitrate.
perturbation.changed_text: The arbitrator(s) shall have the power to grant any remedy or relief that they deem just and equitable, excluding injunctive relief, whether interim and/or final, and any provisional measures ordered by the arbitrator(s) may not be specifically enforced by any court of competent jurisdiction. Each Party hereto waives the right to seek interim measures from a judicial or other governmental authority, and any such request shall be deemed incompatible with the agreement to arbitrate and a waiver of the right to arbitrate.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the arbitrator's ability to grant injunctive relief and preventing provisional measures from being enforced, creates a contradiction with previous sections that rely on the possibility of injunctive relief or provisional measures. This modification directly contradicts the assumption that the arbitrator can provide comprehensive remedies. Here, it directly contradicts, section 14, which allows the parties to get other remedies at law or in equity will in all circumstances be adequate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In any action or proceeding between the Parties (including any arbitration proceeding) arising under or with respect to this Agreement or in any manner pertaining to the Hyatt All-Inclusive Resorts or to the relationship of the Parties under this Agreement, each Party hereby unconditionally and irrevocably waives and releases any right, power or privilege either may have to claim or receive from the other Party any punitive or exemplary damages, each Party acknowledging and agreeing that the remedies herein provided and other remedies at law or in equity will in all circumstances be adequate. Both Parties acknowledge that they are experienced in negotiating agreements of this sort, and have had the advice of counsel in connection with, and fully understand the nature of, the waiver contained in this Section 14.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the arbitrator's ability to grant injunctive relief and enforce provisional measures, while the contradicted text (Section 14) states that remedies at law or in equity (which include injunctive relief) will be adequate. This creates a direct contradiction, as the changed text removes a remedy assumed to be available and adequate in the original agreement, significantly altering the parties' potential recourse and potentially undermining the enforceability of the agreement.
